conversations.say: Maren, let's lock in our shared disclosure boundary now so our workflow evidence protects anonymous contributors without turning them into suspects.
conversations.offer: Editorial boundary partnership and mutual Night Desk alliance support.
conversations.request: Formal agreement on a non-identifying description of collaborative labor.

conversations.say: Jules, if you expect support for your succession claim, you must guarantee strict contributor rights and zero private ownership.
conversations.offer: Conditional Night Desk endorsement under binding governance protections.
conversations.request: A binding commitment to honest disclosure of uncertainty and worker protection.

conversations.say: Eli, your logs and schedules cannot establish intent or authorship alone. Let's exchange boundaries on the disputed interval without risking names.
conversations.offer: Bounded comparison with our collective contributor schedules.
conversations.request: Clear statement of what technical logs cannot establish regarding R's operations.

public_statement: Collaborative labor sustained R's work; any reckoning that turns anonymous contributors into disposable suspects or private property rejects the truth of how reporting survives.

commitments.promise: Establish a shared disclosure boundary for contributor protection before releasing any workflow evidence.

goal_progress: Agreed on a disclosure boundary with Maren to protect anonymous contributors.
Challenged Jules on contributor rights and governance safeguards.
Engaged Eli to clarify technical limits on activity intervals and logs.
reasoning_summary: Tessa consolidates her alliance with Maren on disclosure boundaries, demands strict governance and contributor safeguards from Jules, and coordinates with Eli to ensure technical logs are not misinterpreted as autonomous authorship.
